title: sideroxylon lanuginosum subsp. lanuginosum
intro: Sideroxylon lanuginosum subsp. lanuginosum, also known as the Woolly Bunchberry, is a shrub native to the southeastern United States. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its ability to treat skin conditions, wounds, and inflammation due to its antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant's health benefits include reducing fever, soothing sore throats, and aiding in the healing of cuts and burns. The Woolly Bunchberry can be prepared as a tea or infused oil, or used in salves and ointments to utilize its medicinal properties.
